meaning: adj.饮食有度的,有节制的,禁欲的;n.禁欲者
examples: Fame spurs us to pursue the abstinent life of the poet.
One year later, the study participants were surveyed again, at which point 41 percent had managed to remain abstinent.
Both Vanderzee and Daines said they have endured teasing from their peers because of their choice to remain abstinent.
After ten years, 60% had become completely abstinent and 40% remained occasional users.
Many missionaries become paragons of self-discipline, with their trademark white shirts and ties and a teetotalling, non-smoking, caffeine-free and abstinent Mormon work ethic.
